title: Blasts from the past random posts from jae
display_title: Blasts from the past random posts from jae
wikitext:
[]: Blasts from the past: random posts from j.a.e.             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:01 am I've been surfing thru google-groups' archive of Japan.anime.evangelion, which, back in the day, was the place for serious discussion of NGE. I thought it would be entertaining, and occasionally enlightening, to repost some interesting stuff that I found there. To start off, here's one that's a minor classic: Hi there, I saw your post and am LOST LOST LOST... I just watched Genesis:12 - at the end of the second episode Eva-1 crushes tnat 17th Angel guy, Kaoru ( ? ) and everything is fine... THEN... I pop in Genesis:13... and it all gets confusing... all this tape is is Shinji dreaming about how pathetic he is and how everyone hates him, yada yada yada... There is NO "next episode" thingy, just a "Thanks a lot!"... this was the FINALE!!! Did I BLINK and miss the third impact somewhere? Am I watching "cut up" tapes? ( these are made by ADV and I have all 13 so far... everything was fine up until 13 I suppose... last 2 eps were "End of the World/ Do you love me?" and " The Beast who shouted "I" at the Heart of the World/ Take care of yourself" ) I've seen NO Gendo with Adam in his hand, NO Eva - 5 ( the one with the wings ) NO Super-Rei, NO Yui as Eva - 1... NONE of what I've read on this group that I'd hoped to see.... So... where did I go wrong? Where do I see all this cool stuff I've read? I was happy as a clam up till tape 13 and now I'm just plain ol' lost... this wasn't even an ending. Are these tapes edited? Or have I bought the wrong series? Or has the NEAT stuff I've read only occured in the movies I see mentioned here ( EoE, Rebirth, Death??? ) Please help me out, I feel pretty rotten here after expecting a climax and getting a dream sequence... :( Sean

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:11 am I feel bad for that guy, but...ROFLMAYO!!!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:44 am Anti-Goth wrote: ROFL MAYO !!!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:45 am Heh, I hope someone straightened that out for him.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:46 am Please, please, don't bring up my old j.a.e posts.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Dec 18, 2007 11:57 am Anti-Goth wrote: I feel bad for that guy, but...ROFLMAYO!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Tue Dec 18, 2007 1:14 pm LOL, I'm not Executer guys. xDDD On a (non-NGE) site I go to, someone once said 'LMAONNAISE'. Yah.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Dec 18, 2007 1:18 pm *wipes sweat from brow* whew, good! admins/mods, check his IP to be certain plz

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Tue Dec 18, 2007 1:34 pm lol the curse of mayo has return'd. no wait it never left!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 18, 2007 5:02 pm drinian wrote: Please, please, don't bring up my old j.a.e posts.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Dec 18, 2007 5:17 pm Here's a good one ; the bit about Ritsuko made me lol...[quote=" Anita Bath"]Leonardo Castro wrote: > Japanese Buddhism and The Instrumentality of men project. > This essay focus only on the Instrumentality of men project and > doesn't entirely explain the last two episodes, to do so one would > need to explore the entire series and analyse with some depth concepts > such as: > The emotional and sexual evolution of a child from birth to > adolescence. > Recurrent concepts in anime series throughout the last decades. > Economical situation of Japan in general and of the film industry in > particular. > Several basic aspects of Japanese an Europen society and religion. > etc... <snipped> Naah, you've got it all wrong. Evangelion was actually Anno's elaborate social commentary on Japan's corporate business world. He uses a lot of symbolism, but none of it is religious... Seele - these guys are the unseen, but all-controlling CEOs who are only worried about the bottom line. Gendo - he's the salaryman who Seele regularly chews out for going over budget on the company projects. Shinji - an example of someone who gets a cushy managerial position because his father believes in nepotism. Rei - she represents the typical corporate drone...often a victim of downsizing, and easily replaced. Asuka - she symbolizes the female struggle in a male dominated work environment. Constantly has to prove herself. Ritsuko - the slutty office lady who sleeps with the boss. Misato - represents the stressed out salaryman/woman. Works hard, plays hard. Has become a boozehound, but hasn't hit the karaoke bars yet. Kaji - terminated for stealing office supplies. Kaoru - an auditor from the head office. Sent by Seele to make sure Gendo is accountable for every red yen. Touji - hey, don't worry about losing the arm & leg cause Nerv Inc. has a good HMO! The Angels -(of course) represent all the rival firms, corporate spies, organized crime, and the like that any company must deal with. The Marduk school - to get a position in a big coproration you have to go to the right universities. Motherless Children - a symbolization of the abandonment felt by the children whose mothers have chosen career over family. Yui clearly represents a mother who is totally absorbed in her work. Human Instrumentality - clearly a representation of Japan's business mantra of the 80's..."Learn to think and work as a team, rather than as an individual". And Anno, himself, is clearly the meglo-maniacal Bill Gates figure in all of this. He's offered as many revisions to Evangelion as Gates has to his Windows software. And now both are under gov't scrutiny. Coincidence? ^__- (Hey, if you're gonna analyze this damn show to death, might as well have some fun with it.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 18, 2007 5:28 pm I WANT THAT ONE FOR THE (as yet nonexistent) HUMOR ARCHIVE.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Tue Dec 18, 2007 8:47 pm That. Is. Funny. @ Ritsuko: Anita Bath wrote: Ritsuko - the slutty office genius lady who contributes an outrageous amount to the company but sleeps with the boss, made an intern go lesbian for her, get addicted to their special brand of office coffee, and get shot into an orange-colored swimming pool.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Tue Dec 18, 2007 9:21 pm Kaji-Terminated for stealing office supplies. LMFAO
